name: Territory Heat Map - Backstory Starter
description: Generates a weekly territory heat map digest for each rep, showing which accounts in their territory are heating up (increased inbound, new contacts engaging, meeting frequency rising) versus cooling down (declining engagement, unresponsive contacts). The workflow pulls Backstory engagement data across all accounts in each rep's territory, calculates week-over-week momentum scores, and uses an AI agent to summarize trends and recommend where to focus time. Delivered every Monday to help reps prioritize their week.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch each rep’s assigned accounts, ownership, and territory segmentation so momentum can be calculated account by account. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch each rep’s assigned accounts, ownership, and territory segmentation so momentum can be calculated account by account.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw territory assignments responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze Territory Heat Map

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Territory Heat Map workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Generates a weekly territory heat map digest for each rep, showing which accounts in their territory are heating up (increased inbound, new contacts engaging, meeting frequency rising) versus cooling down (declining engagement, unresponsive contacts). The workflow pulls Backstory engagement data across all accounts in each rep's territory, calculates week-over-week momentum scores, and uses an AI agent to summarize trends and recommend where to focus time. Delivered every Monday to help reps prioritize their week.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the territory heat map use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
🗺️ **Territory Heat Map** — @james.park | Week of Mar 3

🔥 **HEATING UP:**
- **NovaTech** ===$275,000=== | ↑↑↑ Momentum: +340%
- 5 new contacts engaged this week (was 1/week average)
- VP Product requested pricing for enterprise tier — first executive outreach
- Inbound demo request from Director of Engineering
- **Contoso Ltd** ===$150,000=== | ↑↑ Momentum: +180%
- Champion reopened evaluation after 6 weeks dormant
- Downloaded 3 technical docs + attended webinar Tuesday

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Territory Heat Map outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
